topTargets.name: Jordyn Tyson
topTargets.reasoning: Most likely best WR on the board at 1.12 — RSP DoT 84.5 (RSP WR7) and MFL ADP 12.71 align cleanly with this slot. Consensus and RSP both view him as a first-round rookie pick despite boom-bust volatility, and WR is the thinnest position group on the roster (only 4 rostered).

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 86.3 (Starter grade, RSP WR4) but ADP 13.57 means he could slip to 1.12 — exactly the kind of RSP/consensus gap a medium-affinity GM exploits. Versatile flanker/slot fit addresses the WR shortage with first-round rookie value per Waldman.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 84.4, ADP 21.25 makes him a strong 2.12 target. With 10 contracts expiring this year, retooling at TE on a cheap rookie deal is sensible despite TE:5 — most of those TEs are likely on expiring deals given the turnover.

topTargets.name: Nicholas Singleton
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 84.1 with second/third-round rookie value per Waldman, ADP 25.83 — perfect 2.12 or 3.12 fit. High-pedigree Penn State back is a low-risk depth swing as RB contracts roll over.

topTargets.name: Chris Brazzell II
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 84.2 (RSP WR9) but ADP 26.47 — classic RSP-over-consensus value at 3.12 for a medium-affinity blender. George Pickens-like ceiling is exactly the upside swing you take in round 3 with a thin WR room.

capPosture: Tight but workable at $42.6M of $45.0M used ($2.4M space) — rookies likely need taxi treatment to fit, especially the 1.12 base salary; one active-roster slot is open.

wildcard.name: Myles Montgomery
wildcard.reasoning: RSP DoT 84.1 with the explicit 'MOST UNDERRATED skill talent in the 2026 class' tag and likely UDFA — a medium-affinity GM blending RSP signal could grab him with 3.12 as a taxi flier rather than chasing an ADP name.

summary: Vitside Mafia is in a mid-cycle retool with 10 expiring contracts and tight cap — this draft is about cheap rookie deals to backfill churn, not contention pieces. Expect WR-first at 1.12 (Tyson or Concepcion if he slips), a TE or RB swing at 2.12 (Stowers/Singleton), and an RSP-favored upside flier at 3.12. With only $2.4M of cap space, all three picks likely route to taxi at 50% hits to preserve flexibility for veteran re-signings.
